domains.title: Edge caching & CDN

checklist.label: Cache Rules / CDN cache behavior defined per route class (static vs dynamic vs API)
Authenticated and webhook routes bypass shared cache
Public assets use SWR or versioned URLs — not unbounded stale HTML
Documented purge/on-deploy invalidation for marketing pages

directives: Separate cache policies: static (long TTL), API (no-store), personalized (private, no-store).
Never cache Stripe webhook bodies or session cookies at the edge.
Use Cache API / Cache Rules only with explicit cache keys — avoid poisoning via query strings.
retrievalQueries: cloudflare cache rules CDN stale purge

domains.title: Rate limiting & abuse shaping

checklist.label: Per-IP or per-fingerprint limits on auth and public POST routes
Per-account limits on expensive operations (checkout, password reset)
Webhooks validated + idempotent — not counted only by IP
429 responses include Retry-After; clients must backoff

directives: Layer limits: edge (Cloudflare WAF/rate limit) + Worker (KV/Durable Object counters) + app semantics.
Auth endpoints: stricter than static assets; billing endpoints: tier-aware.
Plan for burst traffic — fixed in-memory counters fail across isolates.
retrievalQueries: cloudflare rate limit worker WAF abuse

domains.title: Load distribution & origin protection

checklist.label: API traffic terminates at Workers — not unprotected origin
Health checks exclude dependency cold-start false negatives
Document single-region vs multi-region expectations for D1/KV
Heavy work offloaded to Queues/Workflows — not synchronous request thread

directives: Cloudflare already load-balances Workers globally — design for stateless handlers.
Protect D1: batch writes, avoid hot rows, use read replicas pattern via caching where safe.
Use Queues for email fan-out, webhook side effects, and corpus reindex — never block MCP/tool paths.

domains.title: Load capacity & quotas

checklist.label: Documented expected RPS/peak and Worker CPU time budget per route
D1 query patterns reviewed for N+1 and table scans at scale
KV value size limits and TTLs defined; no unbounded session blobs
Stripe API call rate aligned with checkout/webhook volume
Email send quota and batching for signup spikes

directives: Define SLOs: p95 latency, error rate, and max concurrent checkouts before throttling.
Model 10× spike: auth signup, webhook replay, and marketing traffic hitting same Worker.
Subscribe tier limits (Workers paid, D1 rows, Vectorize queries) in runbook — not discovered in prod.
retrievalQueries: cloudflare D1 limits KV size workers CPU quota

domains.title: Stress & load testing

checklist.label: k6/Artillery/Locust baseline against staging with production-like bindings
Stripe webhook burst + idempotency verified under parallel delivery
Cold-start and cache-miss path measured — not only warm happy path
D1 timeout / KV miss / Resend 429 scenarios exercised

directives: Test auth + checkout + webhook paths separately — combined test hides bottlenecks.
Include 1M-user *growth* scenario: gradual ramp + thundering herd on launch day.
Store last stress report artifact in repo or CI — Iron Cannon flags missing INFRA-STRESS items in T11.
retrievalQueries: load test k6 workers staging webhook burst

domains.title: Observability under load

checklist.label: requestId on all API responses and structured logs
Latency, traffic, errors, saturation dashboards (Workers Analytics + custom)
Alerts on 5xx rate, queue depth, D1 errors, Stripe webhook failures
Incident runbook linked from README — not only in chat history

directives: Log fields: requestId, route, tier, durationMs, outcome code — never secrets or full PII.
Trace cross-service: Worker → D1 → Stripe → Resend with same correlation id.
Pre-define load-shedding: what degrades first (RAG off, non-critical emails) before hard outage.
